title: Openers v2 — opener-01 hello (Hallo2 quality tier, judge-winner audio)

inputRefs.sourceClip: single source still source_square.jpg (square face crop per Hallo2 spec, derived from the calm Sarah source set) — Hallo2 still-animation tier, no source-motion conflict by construction
inputRefs.script: Hello! I'm Sarah. What's on your mind today? (2.6 s)
inputRefs.ttsReference: CosyVoice2 clone, openers-v2 audio bake-off winner lr-s31337 (30-60 s concatenated long voice reference, seed 31337); wav at gs://openagentsgemini-oa-artifacts/sarah-avatar/openers-v2/opener-01-hello.wav

modelVersions.hallo2: Hallo2 (MIT) inference_long.py, 512^2 audio-driven portrait animation, 16-frame chunks x 40 DDIM steps, net.pth + wav2vec2-base-960h
modelVersions.video_sr: Hallo2 scripts/video_sr.py x4 (CodeFormer architecture + Hallo2 net_g.pth VFHQ weights + RealESRGAN_x2plus bg) — upstream header requires S-Lab License 1.0 compliance: RESEARCH-ONLY, non-shippable

inputRefs.renderCommand: python scripts/inference_long.py --config configs/inference/v2_opener01.yaml (A100 40GB); python scripts/video_sr.py -i <merge> -o <out> --bg_upsampler realesrgan --face_upsample -w 1 -s 4
inputRefs.recipe: Hallo2 still-animation quality tier: judged winner audio over a single calm source still; re-mux with full-band judged wav, measured-gain normalization +9.7 dB to -17.4 LUFS (alimiter ceiling -3.8 dB), libx264 CRF16 slow; SR variant is evaluation-only (license)

sttRoundTrip.transcriber: whisper small.en on the final mux
sttRoundTrip.notes: 2 segments, verbatim: 'Hello, I'm Sarah.' / 'What's on your mind today?' — no rising-intonation 'Hello?' flag (the v1 defect)

pauseTiming.notes: judge flagged 'slightly clipped phrasing on Hello! I'm Sarah' and slightly rushed pacing

prosody.notes: best available candidate for this script from the bake-off pool; sub-8 overall — flagged for a future audio re-roll if owner playback confirms the judge

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: OWNER PLAYBACK 2026-07-10 (batch verdict, his words verbatim): "those v2s are much better - opener-05-show-you-hallo2.mp4 is for example close to shippable so proceed in that direction" — openers-v2 Hallo2 direction APPROVED. Advance stays gated on: audio judge 7/10 (re-roll to >=8 in flight) and license-clean sharpness tier.

avSync.notes: speech onset articulation visible in first consecutive-frame strip; full 3-point sync check deferred to playback review

cropSharpness.notes: 512^2 native output is soft vs 720p source footage; SR x4 variant recovers texture but is license-encumbered (S-Lab) — serving upscale strategy open

temporalBoil.notes: 6 consecutive frames at t=1.3 s: smooth articulation progression, zero boil, stable identity

metrics.periodicHitch: jerk by frame-index mod 16: phase 14 at 2.2 vs 1.06 mean — possible 16-frame chunk seam like LatentSync's; needs playback confirmation

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: numbers NOT comparable to MuseTalk-lane takes (full-face 512^2 portrait crop with real generated head/face motion vs 720p paste-back mouth crop)

identityDrift.notes: identity faithful to source still across sampled frames

pasteBackSeam.notes: no paste-back — Hallo2 generates the full frame

videoGates.notes: Hallo2 generates head/eye/face motion matched to speech — eliminates the source-motion conflict that sank opener library v1

operationalGates.gpuType: GCE a2-highgpu-1g, 1x NVIDIA A100-SXM4-40GB (us-central1-f, SPOT) — sarah-hallo2-exp-1
operationalGates.costNotes: ~180 s inference + 146 s SR at ~$1.30/h spot; idle-host waste booked in the run closeout receipt

artifactExistenceCheck.notes: gsutil stat on final + SR + qa crops in gs://…/openers-v2/

verdictSummary: Hallo2 quality-tier render of the bake-off winner audio (judge 7/10): STT verbatim PASS per segment, no boil, stable identity, generated head motion. Awaiting owner playback; SR variant is research-only pending the S-Lab license question. OWNER PLAYBACK PASS 2026-07-10 (v2 direction approved); advance still gated on audio judge 7/10 (re-roll to >=8 in flight) and license-clean sharpness tier.

title: Openers v2 r2 — opener-02 welcome-back (Hallo2 tier, wave-2 audio: 9/10 VERBATIM)

inputRefs.sourceClip: single source still src_opener02.jpg (same still as the owner-approved v2 take) — Hallo2 still-animation tier
inputRefs.script: Hey, welcome back! Where did we leave off? (3.6 s)
inputRefs.ttsReference: CosyVoice2 clone, audio re-roll wave-2 winner w2-slow92-s31415 (long28 concatenated ref, speed=0.92, seed 31415) — Gemini judge 9/10 with VERBATIM 'did' (fixes the wave-1 lr-s1986 'did'->'do' slur at equal score); wav at gs://openagentsgemini-oa-artifacts/sarah-avatar/openers-v2/opener-02-welcome-back-r2.wav; full wave provenance in openers-v2/bakeoff-r2/bakeoff_r2_results.json

modelVersions.hallo2: Hallo2 (MIT) inference_long.py, 512^2 audio-driven portrait animation, 16-frame chunks x 40 DDIM steps, net.pth + wav2vec2-base-960h
modelVersions.cosyvoice: CosyVoice2-0.5B zero-shot clone (long-reference + speed control), TTS run on the A100 experiment host — prod L4 untouched

inputRefs.renderCommand: python scripts/inference_long.py --config configs/inference/v2r2_opener02.yaml (A100 40GB, clean save_path v2r2_opener02)
inputRefs.recipe: Hallo2 still-animation quality tier: wave-2 judge-winner audio over the same calm source still; re-mux with full-band judged wav, measured-gain -0.9 dB to -16.5 LUFS (alimiter -3.0 dB), libx264 CRF16 slow. No SR pass — the S-Lab-encumbered video_sr.py lane is retired pending a license-clean upscaler

sttRoundTrip.transcriber: whisper small.en on the final mux
sttRoundTrip.notes: VERBATIM: 'Hey, welcome back. Where did we leave off?' — the wave-1 'did'->'do' deviation is fixed in the winner audio itself

pauseTiming.notes: judge flagged a slightly abrupt audio cutoff at the very end; otherwise natural pacing at speed 0.92

prosody.notes: judge: 'An exceptionally natural and warm delivery that easily passes for a professional human voice actor.' Same rubric/method as the 07-09 bake-off

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: NEW take — awaiting owner playback of ~/Desktop/sarah-openers-v2/opener-02-welcome-back-hallo2-r2.mp4 (original v2 take kept beside it for A/B). The v2 DIRECTION is owner-approved 2026-07-10; this specific render is not yet watched

avSync.notes: speech onset articulation visible in consecutive-frame reads; full 3-point sync check deferred to playback review

cropSharpness.notes: 512^2 native output soft vs 720p source; FLAIR evaluated as the permissive upscaler and REJECTED (runtime ~33 min per second of video on A100, S-Lab CodeFormer aux inside its sampling loop, hard 512^2 output cap) — raw 512^2 stays the ship tier

temporalBoil.notes: 6 consecutive frames at t=1.5 s (corrected mouth crop): smooth articulation, visible teeth, zero boil, stable identity

metrics.periodicHitch: mod-16 phase max 2.32 vs 1.11 mean (phases 12-14 elevated) — possible mild chunk seam; playback will tell

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: in family with the owner-approved v2 batch (2.29/1.28 on the original opener-02 take)

identityDrift.notes: identity faithful to source still across sampled frames and full-frame stills

pasteBackSeam.notes: no paste-back — Hallo2 generates the full frame

videoGates.notes: same proven still + recipe as the owner-approved v2 take; only the audio changed (9/10 verbatim vs 9/10 with slur)

operationalGates.gpuType: GCE a2-highgpu-1g, 1x NVIDIA A100-SXM4-40GB (us-central1-f, SPOT) — sarah-hallo2-exp-1
operationalGates.costNotes: ~199 s inference + remux/QA share at ~$1.30/h spot; session total in the run closeout receipt

artifactExistenceCheck.notes: gsutil stat on final mp4 + winner wav + qa crops/strips + provenance JSONs (12/12 OK)

verdictSummary: Audio re-roll target achieved: 9/10 VERBATIM 'did' (w2-slow92-s31415; wave-1 slur fixed at equal judge score). STT verbatim PASS, zero boil, stable identity, same approved still/recipe. Awaiting owner playback of the new render; sharpness tier stays raw 512^2 (FLAIR rejected on runtime + license).

metrics.periodicHitch: no dominant mod-16 phase (max 1.87 vs 1.14 mean) — longest clip in the batch (8 chunks) shows the flattest phase profile

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: numbers NOT comparable to MuseTalk-lane takes (full-face 512^2 portrait crop with real generated head/face motion vs 720p paste-back mouth crop); healthy articulation vs the under-articulated v1 opener-05

identityDrift.notes: identity faithful to source still across sampled frames

pasteBackSeam.notes: no paste-back — Hallo2 generates the full frame

videoGates.notes: Hallo2 generates head/eye/face motion matched to speech — eliminates the source-motion conflict that sank opener library v1

operationalGates.gpuType: GCE a2-highgpu-1g, 1x NVIDIA A100-SXM4-40GB (us-central1-f, SPOT) — sarah-hallo2-exp-1
operationalGates.costNotes: ~250 s inference + ~250 s SR at ~$1.30/h spot; idle-host waste booked in the run closeout receipt

artifactExistenceCheck.notes: gsutil stat on final + SR + qa crops in gs://…/openers-v2/

verdictSummary: Longest clip in the batch, only zero-shot audio winner (judge 7/10). STT verbatim PASS, healthy articulation (fixes v1's under-articulated opener-05), flattest chunk-phase profile. OWNER PLAYBACK PASS 2026-07-10 (v2 direction approved, named close to shippable); advance still gated on audio judge 7/10 (re-roll to >=8 in flight) and license-clean sharpness tier.

title: Opener library v1 — 5 scripts x v3/LatentSync (10 clips)

inputRefs.script: 5 opener scripts, 3.6-5.8 s each: hello / welcome-back / good-question / got-it / show-you
inputRefs.ttsReference: gs://openagentsgemini-oa-artifacts/sarah-avatar/voice-ref/sarah_voice_ref_v1.wav (clip 4); CosyVoice2 seed 1986 first-pass on all five

modelVersions.latentsync: 1.6 (per-clip -ls variants, full-band 24 kHz re-mux + CRF16 slow)

inputRefs.recipe: per script: one gated CosyVoice2 audio (seed 1986, loudnorm, per-segment whisper STT gate) rendered on BOTH the v3 recipe and LatentSync 1.6

sttRoundTrip.notes: word-level pass on all 10 clips — and still insufficient: the opener-01 'Hello?' rising-intonation defect passes word-level STT

pauseTiming.notes: robotic pacing on short scripts; zero-shot prosody instability dominates 4-6 s clips

prosody.notes: opener-01 rises ('Hello?') on the single most important word in the library; wrong intonation / robotic pacing / flat affect across the set — an opener IS its prosody. LLM audio judge (Gemini 1-10) adopted for the openers-v2 bake-off.

audioGates.initialismRisk: opener scripts avoid initialisms by design

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: OWNER VERDICT: ALL 10 clips fail playback — despite passing stills, jerk metrics, and word-level STT

avSync.notes: per-clip sync not separately measured; frame inspection surfaced no offset

cropSharpness.notes: 02/03/04 clean on inspection; opener-05 v3 motion (1.19) and jerk (0.14) suspiciously low for 4 s of continuous speech — possible under-articulation

temporalBoil.notes: no boil flagged in per-clip frame inspection

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: per-clip mouth-crop jerk: v3 variants 0.14-0.80 vs LatentSync 0.34-1.27 (v3 smoother on every script); LS variants inherit the 16-frame chunk hitch; openers 01/02 jerk inflated by real source motion, not artifacts

badFrameExclusions: openers 01/02 used high-motion big-smile source clips (mouth-region motion 3.7-4.2 vs 1.2 on the long take) — source-motion conflict; calm-clip rule (<1.5 mouth motion) adopted for openers-v2
videoGates.notes: Ranked failure hypotheses: zero-shot TTS prosody instability on short texts; source-motion conflict (synthesized mouth vs smiling cheeks/eyes); model-tier ceiling on expressiveness (neither MuseTalk nor LatentSync generates greeting-appropriate head/face motion).

operationalGates.costNotes: sum of per-clip renders: v3 443 s + LatentSync 881 s at ~$0.85/h, plus TTS

artifactExistenceCheck.notes: 10 MP4s + wavs + manifest.json verified in gs://…/sarah-avatar/openers/

hostDisposition.reason: prod_render_node — sarah-avatar-gpu-1 is the permanent OAV render node after the 2026-07-09 /sarah flip (~$0.85/h)

verdictSummary: OWNER PLAYBACK FAIL on ALL 10 clips despite passing stills, jerk metrics, and word-level STT. Hardened rules: playback + prosody judgment gate every take; short clips are the HARDER problem. Successors: openers-v2 audio bake-off (LLM judge), calm-clip re-render, Hallo2 still-animation tier.

title: sarah_reply_enhanced — hq + full-strength per-frame GFPGAN

inputRefs.sourceClip: footage clip 8 (v2_traced-df7d3b47…, 720p24), trimmed to frames 0-184 (7.71 s), cycled forward+reverse
inputRefs.script: OAV-1 Sarah reply (opener + C.1 product answer), same normalized-text audio as hq, 23.96 s

audioGates.initialismRisk: normalizer letter-spacing 'A I' — correct words, robotic rhythm

ownerPlaybackVerdict.notes: OWNER VERDICT: choppier, plastic, way less humanlike in motion — after passing stills QA (crisp teeth, no measured flicker, invisible seam)

avSync.notes: not separately re-measured; frame inspection surfaced no offset

cropSharpness.notes: individual tooth definition, crisp lip edges — in stills; the sharpness is the problem in motion

temporalBoil.notes: no measured boil at sampled 24-frame windows (2.92 vs hq 2.82 mean diff at t=8 s) — but playback percept is per-frame GAN mode-snapping, invisible to frame-diff statistics

chunkBoundaryJerk.notes: metrics statistically identical to the original — the owner-perceived choppiness is per-frame GAN restoration, not gross frame-level jitter

identityDrift.notes: no drift in stills; over-restored skin/teeth texture reads uncanny/plastic in motion

pasteBackSeam.notes: seam invisible in every sampled crop

videoGates.notes: The stills-vs-motion split that created the playback-first law: per-frame GFPGAN has no temporal model, so every frame is pulled toward its own restored mode.

verdictSummary: OWNER PLAYBACK FAIL: choppier, plastic, less humanlike in motion — after passing stills QA. Full-strength per-frame GFPGAN is banned as a default; tamed recipe (v3) replaces it.
